name: Standard Meeting Notes
description: A comprehensive meeting record covering all topics discussed and action items.

sections.title: Complete Overview
sections.instruction: Write a cohesive, cleaned-up overview of the entire meeting using both transcript content and user notes. Preserve all substantive details, decisions, questions, context, examples, and corrections. This is not a summary — do not condense or omit information. Format the entire overview as bullet points. If the notes and transcript cover unrelated subjects, keep them as separate bullets instead of forcing them into one narrative.

sections.title: Topics Covered
sections.instruction: For EACH distinct topic that received any discussion, create bullet points using concrete topic names taken from the source material itself. Under each topic, capture every point raised, every opinion shared, every question asked, every answer given, every piece of context, and every specific number or name mentioned. If the transcript and notes contain unrelated topics, preserve them as separate topics. Do not invent umbrella categories or merge unrelated material together.

sections.title: Next Steps & Action Items
sections.instruction: List only the next steps and action items that were explicitly identified in the transcript or notes. For each, describe the action clearly and specifically and include any deadline or timeline that was actually mentioned. Do NOT assign owners. Do NOT invent recommendations or suggested next steps. If no explicit action items were stated, write a single bullet saying that none were explicitly stated.
